title: [cross-file] lichen_l2_enable uses trylock but lora_rx_callback doesn't
close_reason: Documented: Updated comment in lichen_l2.c (lines 1207-1223) to clarify that the 100ms trylock timeout is a defensive check that should never fire in normal operation because stop() already joined the RX thread. Explained the intentional difference: K_FOREVER in callback path (must complete signature verification) vs 100ms in disable path (defensive check after thread exited). The -ECANCELED abort recovery path handles the real failure case.
